composite_combine_desc: Pick how the multiple sources combine into one shelf. Union shows everything from any source; Intersection shows only games present in every source.
decoration_image_pick_hint: Choose an image from this device
decoration_intro: Add decorative cards, placeholders, or focusable shortcuts at fixed slots of this shelf.
docs_filter_achievements_desc: Filter by achievement count or completion. Requires a compatible plugin.
docs_filter_app_status_desc: Show only games with a specific app status: actively downloading or applying an update, update queued or paused, initial install in progress, or currently running.
docs_filter_appidlist_desc: Whitelist an explicit set of Steam app IDs. Only those games appear in the shelf.
docs_filter_collection_desc: Show games that belong to a specific Steam collection.
docs_filter_compat_desc: Filter by Steam Deck compatibility level: Verified, Playable, Unsupported, or Unknown.
docs_filter_controller_support_desc: Filter by controller support level (partial / full).
docs_filter_developer_desc: Show games developed by specific studios. Options are discovered from your library automatically.
docs_filter_discount_desc: Steam store discount percentage in a chosen range. Requires online features.
docs_filter_favorites_desc: Show only games marked as favourites in your Steam library.
docs_filter_friends_desc: Show games played by your friends. Requires a compatible plugin for friend data.
docs_filter_friends_played_recently_desc: Game a Steam friend played within the last N days (1-30, default 14). Requires online features.
docs_filter_friends_playing_now_desc: Game any Steam friend is currently in-game on. Requires online features.
docs_filter_hidden_desc: Filter by visibility: show all, hidden only, or exclude hidden games.
docs_filter_installed_desc: Show only games currently installed on this device.
docs_filter_is_new_desc: Match games added to your library in the last 14 days.
docs_filter_merge_desc: Combine multiple sub-filters into a single nested group with its own AND/OR logic.
docs_filter_name_includes_desc: Show games whose name contains the specified text (case-insensitive).
docs_filter_name_regex_desc: Show games whose name matches a regular expression pattern.
docs_filter_nonsteam_desc: Show only non-Steam games (shortcuts added manually or via other launchers).
docs_filter_played_within_desc: Show games played within the last N days.
docs_filter_playtime_range_desc: Show games with total playtime between a minimum and maximum (in minutes).
docs_filter_publisher_desc: Show games published by specific publishers. Options are discovered from your library automatically.
docs_filter_shortcut_type_desc: Filter by entry type: Games (Steam titles), Software (Steam applications), Tools (Proton, runtimes, redistributables), or Non-Steam links (shortcuts added outside Steam).
docs_filter_store_tag_desc: Filter by Steam Store tags (e.g. RPG, Indie, Action). Requires tag data from Steam.
docs_filter_update_pending_desc: Show only games with a pending update or download.
docs_filters_and_or_tip: Use AND when every condition must hold (e.g. installed + Deck Verified). Use OR when any condition is enough (e.g. one developer or another).
docs_filters_groups_desc: Filters can be combined using AND (all must match) or OR (any must match) logic. You can also invert individual filters to exclude matches, and nest groups using the Merge filter for complex queries.
docs_filters_intro: When using a filter source, you can combine multiple criteria to narrow down which games appear. Available filter options:
docs_filters_invert_tip: Most filters can be inverted — combine with AND to exclude specific subsets, e.g. installed games that are not hidden.
docs_filters_pending_note: Store Tag, Friends, and Achievements filters are saved and exported correctly but are not yet evaluated at runtime — shelves using only these filters will show all library games.
docs_filters_sort_body: Games can be sorted alphabetically by name or by most recent play session.
docs_filters_sort_tab_note: Sort has no effect on Tab-based shelves (Favourites, Recent, Installed) — order for those comes directly from Steam.
docs_howto_note_smart: Smart Shelves appear and disappear automatically based on your library. Enable them from the Smart Shelves section in the QAM.
docs_howto_note_footer: Need more help? Use the Quick Search overlay (L1+R1) to jump straight to any game across your shelves, or open the dedicated Settings page from the gear icon in the QAM to manage profiles, integrations and backups in one place.
docs_howto_tip: Start with a template — it sets up the source and sort for you. You can rename and adjust everything after creation.
docs_online_body: Opt-in online features extend Deck Shelves with data from the Steam Store: your wishlist, currently discounted titles, and price/discount sorts. All requests are demand-driven (never polled) and cached locally so the home shelves keep working offline.
docs_online_discount_badge_body: Cards in online shelves show a green badge (e.g. "75% off") in the same slot as the NEW badge whenever the price cache reports a non-zero discount. Also rendered on placeholder cards so the badge is visible even when artwork is still loading.
docs_online_enable_body: Open the QAM, expand the new "Additional features" section and turn on "Online features". A privacy banner appears on first enable listing every URL contacted and what's sent — no personal data beyond the public Steam ID. Sub-toggles for Wishlist, Price/Discount sort and "Exclude owned games" appear beneath.
docs_online_exclude_body: Per-shelf toggle on wishlist/store sources. Hides games whose exact name matches a title in your local library — including non-Steam shortcuts that share a name with a Steam Store entry but use a different appid.
docs_online_privacy_note: No telemetry, no third-party domains. Requests only go to store.steampowered.com / steamcommunity.com / api.github.com (for update checks).
docs_online_refresh_body: Online shelves expose a "Refresh cache" entry in the QAM action menu, in the shelf-card context menu, and on the trailing refresh card. Clears the wishlist / store / price / name caches and re-resolves the shelf. Random and smart shelves get an equivalent "Refresh" action that re-shuffles the source without touching caches.
docs_online_refresh_tip: Card art for some newly-released games sometimes lacks the standard library_600x900 portrait. The fallback chain tries every common CDN variant (library_600x900_2x, capsule_*, header.jpg) before showing a placeholder — and the placeholder still renders the discount badge.
docs_online_sort_original_price_high_des: highest original price first (best deal value)
docs_online_source_store_desc: specials + free + popular pages of the Steam Store; cached 6 h
docs_online_source_wishlist_desc: your Steam Store wishlist; cached 24 h
docs_online_template_free_now_desc: any Steam Store game temporarily free — wishlist not required
docs_online_template_wishlist_on_sale_de: wishlisted games with 1–99% discount, sorted by discount
docs_overview_footer_explore: Each of the tabs above goes deeper into one specific area — open Shelves for templates and management, Filters for the full filter type list, Smart shelves for every heuristic mode, or Sort for ordering options.
docs_overview_footer_support_hint: Project links, version info, known limitations and how to support development live in the Support tab.
docs_shelves_child_filter_tip: When using a Collection or Tab source, an Additional Filters tab appears in the editor to narrow the results further with the same filter options available in the Filters tab.
docs_shelves_decoration_body: Pin fixed-slot cards inside a shelf row: a text label, an image banner, a focusable URL shortcut, or a transparent gap that focus skips over. Each card is added at the slot you focused in the preview; the shelf auto-switches to manual sort so you can drag the card around later.
docs_shelves_decoration_tip: Text and image are mutually exclusive. A link only attaches when the card has text or an image — pure gaps stay non-focusable.
docs_shelves_display_options_body: The Display tab of each shelf editor lets you: deduplicate entries by exact name (Steam wins over non-Steam), manually exclude specific games from a shelf, and toggle per-shelf visibility of card badges, status line, game names, shelf title, and trailing cards.
docs_shelves_intro: Shelves are customizable rows of games displayed on your Steam Deck Home screen. Each shelf pulls games from a source you choose.
docs_shelves_manage_body: Use the action menu (⋯) on each shelf to edit, duplicate, reorder, show/hide, or delete it. You can also import and export your complete shelf configuration as a JSON file.
docs_shelves_multi_source_body: A shelf can stack more than one source. Pick a primary source (collection, tab, wishlist, store, filter), then click "Add source" to add extras. Two or more sources collapse into a composite on save — Union shows games appearing in any of them; Intersection shows games appearing in every one.
docs_shelves_multi_source_tip: You can not stack two identical sources on the same shelf (e.g. Collection A + Collection A). Once a collection, tab, wishlist, or store is in use, it disappears from the picker. Filter is mutually exclusive with the other source types — use a filter merge for multi-criteria predicates.
docs_shelves_quickactions_body: Y on any card toggles the per-card highlight. Every card menu (DS shelves and native library) carries "Add to shelf" — the list only shows shelves that still have room and don't already contain the game (per-shelf limit + 50-card cap).
docs_shelves_source_collection: Shows games from a Steam collection you've created.
docs_shelves_source_filter: Shows games matching custom filter criteria.
docs_shelves_source_tab: Shows games from a library section (e.g. Recent, Favourites).
docs_shelves_template_tip: All templates produce fully editable shelves — source, sort, limit, and display options can all be changed after creation.
docs_smart_day_overrides: Toggle in the Smart filters tab. When on, an Overrides tab appears where you can give specific weekdays their own hour ranges — overriding the default hours for those days only.
docs_smart_filters_overview: The Smart filters tab gathers everything that's specific to the chosen mode: tuning sliders, the visibility window with one or more hour ranges, and the days of the week the shelf is visible. The Source tab keeps the basics (title, sort, limit, refresh interval).
docs_smart_param_lookback: How far back the time-window heuristics look (Recently Played: days; Rediscover: months; Forgotten: years).
docs_smart_param_min_deck_level: Minimum Steam Deck compatibility level the candidates must reach. Pick 'Any' to skip the filter, 'Verified only' to keep just the green ones.
docs_smart_param_playtime: Max / min playtime thresholds. Defaults are sensible per template (e.g. Quick Play caps at 120 min); raise or lower to fit your habit.
docs_smart_params_overview: Each smart shelf exposes a few tuning knobs in the Smart filters tab. Change these to adapt the heuristic without giving up the template.
docs_smart_refresh_interval: How often the shelf recomputes its candidates. Default 60 minutes; raise it for stability or lower it for fresher rotations.
docs_smart_shelves_enable: Enable Smart Shelves in the QAM to add them. Each template has a different heuristic.
docs_smart_shelves_hide_tip: If a shelf rarely matches your library, prefer hiding it over deleting — it can be re-enabled later without losing its position.
docs_smart_shelves_intro: Smart shelves are generated automatically from your library. They appear when games match the criteria and disappear when none do — no manual configuration needed.
docs_smart_sort_almost_finished: Sort: achievement % (high to low). Best-effort — fills as Steam data caches.
docs_smart_sort_backlog_rescue: Sort: weighted (playtime + staleness + Deck level), with 14-day cooldown.
docs_smart_sort_coop_ready: Sort: last session. Best-effort — depends on Steam category cache.
docs_smart_sort_couch_gaming: Sort: last session. Best-effort — depends on Steam category cache.
docs_smart_sort_daily_pick: Sort: deterministic by date (changes each day)
docs_smart_sort_forgotten_gems: Sort: review %, then metacritic, then acquisition date.
docs_smart_sort_friends_playing: Sort: currently in-game first, then by your last session. Online-gated.
docs_smart_sort_long_session_night: Sort: total playtime (high to low). Visible 19h–23h by default.
docs_smart_sort_low_battery_mode: Sort: when battery low → smallest first; otherwise Deck level + last session.
docs_smart_sort_monthly_spotlight: Sort: round-robin over installed games; advances once per 30 days.
docs_smart_sort_party_games: Sort: last session. Best-effort — depends on Steam category cache.
docs_smart_sort_rediscover: Sort: time since last session (longest gap first)
docs_smart_sort_seasonal_rotation: Sort: round-robin over installed games; advances once per 90 days.
docs_smart_sort_short_battery: Sort: Deck level, then last session.
docs_smart_sort_spare_time: Sort: alphabetical; only shown in short-session time windows
docs_smart_sort_time_of_day: Sort: changes by hour of day
docs_smart_sort_weekly_rotation: Sort: round-robin over installed games; advances once per week.
docs_smart_surprise_me: Surprise Me: the system picks 1–5 smart shelf templates each day automatically. You can set an exact count or leave it at 0 for the system to decide.
docs_smart_template_almost_finished: Games with achievement progress ≥ threshold (default 70%) — close to completion. Best-effort: depends on Steam achievements being cached for your library; first paint may be partial.
docs_smart_template_backlog_rescue: Installed games with some playtime that you haven't touched in 30+ days. Rotates with a 14-day cooldown so the same game doesn't keep resurfacing.
docs_smart_template_best_unplayed: Installed games you have never played.
docs_smart_template_coop_ready: Games tagged with Co-op or Online Co-op. Best-effort: depends on Steam store categories being cached for each app.
docs_smart_template_couch_gaming: Games tagged with Shared/Split Screen multi-player. Best-effort: depends on Steam store categories being cached for each app.
docs_smart_template_daily_pick: A different set of games each day — deterministic, based on date.
docs_smart_template_deck_picks: Deck Verified titles in your library, sorted by last played.
docs_smart_template_forgotten: Steam games owned for 3+ years that you have never launched — sorted from oldest acquisition to newest.
docs_smart_template_forgotten_gems: Owned games you've never played that have high review percentage or metacritic score. Ranked by review score, then metacritic, then acquisition recency.
docs_smart_template_friends_playing: Games your Steam friends are playing right now (or, optionally, played in the last 14 days). Online-gated: only shows when Online features are enabled. Returns empty when no friend has matching activity yet.
docs_smart_template_hidden_gems: Owned games with high review percentage (≥85%) that you've never played — overlooked titles in your library.
docs_smart_template_interrupted: Games with 30 min to 3 hours of playtime — started but not committed.
docs_smart_template_long_session: Installed games with over 3 hours of playtime — your committed titles.
docs_smart_template_long_session_night: Installed games with over 3 hours of playtime, surfaced only between 19h and 23h — committed titles for evening sessions.
docs_smart_template_low_battery_mode: Lightweight Deck-friendly games. When the deck is on battery below the threshold, surfaces the smallest + shortest-playtime titles first. On AC / unknown battery, behaves like Short battery session.
docs_smart_template_monthly_spotlight: Round-robin slice over your installed games, advancing once per 30 days. Long-running spotlight rotation.
docs_smart_template_never_touched_classi: Steam games acquired 3+ years ago that you've never launched. Surfaces oldest acquisitions first.
docs_smart_template_non_steam: Non-Steam shortcuts and emulator entries, sorted by last played.
docs_smart_template_not_started: Steam games with zero playtime that have never been launched.
docs_smart_template_on_deck: Installed games that are Deck Verified or Playable, sorted by recently played.
docs_smart_template_party_games: Games tagged with Local Multi-Player / Local PvP. Best-effort: depends on Steam store categories being cached for each app.
docs_smart_template_quick_play: Installed Deck-compatible games with less than 2 hours of playtime.
docs_smart_template_random_pick: Random selection from your entire library — order refreshes every 24 hours.
docs_smart_template_recent_hidden_instal: Installed in the last 30 days but never launched — catches recent acquisitions that fell through the cracks.
docs_smart_template_recently_played: Games you've played in the last 30 days.
docs_smart_template_rediscover: Deck-compatible games with over 1 hour of playtime that you haven't touched in 6+ months.
docs_smart_template_seasonal_rotation: Round-robin slice over your installed games, advancing once per 90 days (one season). Slow, deliberate rotation for long-term curation.
docs_smart_template_short_battery: Installed Deck-friendly games small in install size (≤4 GB) and short in playtime (≤2 hours) — quick play that won't drain the battery.
docs_smart_template_spare_time: Installed games with ≤2 hours of playtime, shown during short-session windows: 6–9h, 12–14h, and 19–22h. Hidden outside those windows.
docs_smart_template_time_of_day: Changes based on the hour: Quick Play in the morning, Deck Picks in the afternoon, Rediscover at night.
docs_smart_template_travel_mode: Installed Deck-friendly games that fit in modest storage (≤5 GB). Useful when packing a microSD for a trip.
docs_smart_template_weekly_rotation: Round-robin slice over your installed games, advancing once per week. Same slice all week, then rotates to the next batch.
docs_smart_time_of_day_rotation: The Time of Day shelf rotates its content automatically: Quick Play in the morning (05–12), Deck Picks in the afternoon (12–18), Rediscover in the evening / night (18–05). The shelf itself stays visible all day; only the games change.
docs_smart_visibility_days: Pick which weekdays the shelf is visible. All checked = no restriction. Empty = never visible.
docs_smart_visibility_per_day: Each individual time range can also specify which days of the week it applies to — independently from the shelf-level day filter. Ranges without a day restriction follow the shelf-level day setting.
docs_smart_visibility_window: Pick one or more hour ranges and (optionally) restrict to specific weekdays. The shelf disappears from the home outside the window. Spare Time shelves come pre-filled with their built-in windows; Time of Day surfaces its inner content-rotation boundaries as read-only context.
docs_sort_added_desc: Sort by when the game was added to your library (newest first).
docs_sort_alpha_desc: Sort games alphabetically by display name.
docs_sort_direction_body: Every sort defaults to descending order (newest / largest / highest first). Each shelf can flip its own sort to ascending without changing the source list.
docs_sort_direction_tip: Use the icon button next to the sort dropdown to flip between descending (default) and ascending — works for every sort except Manual and Random.
docs_sort_multikey_body: Example: "Discount %" + "Metacritic" orders by biggest discount first; games at the same discount fall back to the metacritic score. The chain is stable, so the secondary key only kicks in when the primary genuinely ties — there is no quiet re-shuffle. Manual and Random can only be the single primary key, never tiebreakers.
docs_sort_multikey_tip: Click "Add secondary sort" to chain a tiebreaker. The first key drives the order; subsequent keys break ties when the primary value is equal. Each row has its own asc/desc toggle.
docs_sort_playtime_desc: Sort by total playtime (most played first).
docs_sort_recent_desc: Sort by most recent play session (last played first).
docs_sort_release_date_desc: Sort by original release date (newest first).
docs_sort_review_desc: Sort by Steam user review score (highest first).
docs_sort_size_desc: Sort by install size on disk (largest first).
docs_template_awaiting_update: Games with a pending update or download.
docs_template_blank: An empty shelf — opens the edit form to configure from scratch.
docs_template_deck_verified: Games rated Deck Verified — Valve confirmed they work great on Steam Deck.
docs_template_favorites: Games marked as favourites in your Steam library.
docs_template_installed: All games currently installed on your device.
docs_template_long_session: Installed games with more than 3 hours of playtime — your committed titles.
docs_template_most_played: Your most-played games sorted by total playtime.
docs_template_non_steam: Non-Steam games and emulator shortcuts in your library.
docs_template_recent: Games you have played most recently, sorted by last session.
docs_template_recently_added: Games recently added to your Steam library.
docs_template_steam_cloud: Games with Steam Cloud support — saves sync across devices.
docs_template_top_reviewed: Installed games with the highest user review scores on Steam.
filter_achievements_info: Filters by achievements (requires a compatible plugin)
filter_finish_before_adding: Please finish the current filter before adding another
filter_friends_info: Shows games played by your friends (requires a compatible plugin)
filter_friends_played_recently_info: Matches games at least one Steam friend was seen playing in the lookback window. Includes friends currently playing. Requires Online features.
filter_friends_playing_now_info: Matches games at least one Steam friend is currently in-game on. Requires Online features.
filter_publisher_empty: Publisher data is not exposed by the SteamOS Big Picture API; this filter will not match any games.
first_run_desc: Create custom game shelves on your Home screen. Start with defaults or add shelves manually.
import_from_tabmaster_desc: Import your TabMaster library tabs and convert them into shelves.
online_features_desc: Allows shelves to use Steam Store data (wishlist, prices). Only URLs listed below are contacted.
online_privacy_body: Deck Shelves will connect to the Steam Store to fetch your wishlist and game prices — the same data Steam already uses. Nothing new is shared, and no account credentials are ever sent. You can turn this off at any time.
online_privacy_item_ping: Connection check — only before fetching store data
online_privacy_item_price: Game prices — cached for 6 hours, only when needed
reset_all_confirm_desc: This permanently deletes all shelves, settings and cached data for Deck Shelves. This cannot be undone.
reset_shelves_confirm_desc: Delete all shelves? This cannot be undone.
reset_smart_shelves_confirm_desc: Delete all smart shelves and disable smart features? This cannot be undone.
smart_allow_day_overrides_desc: Set different hour ranges for specific days of the week
smart_composite_desc: Mix multiple smart modes into one shelf. Union = any mode matches. Intersection = all modes match.
smart_first_run_desc: Smart shelves appear automatically based on your library. They disappear when no games match.
smart_refresh_interval_desc: How often to recompute this shelf, in minutes. Default: 60 min.
smart_template_custom_desc: Build your own smart shelf from scratch — set the filters and sort manually.
smart_template_picker_desc: Smart shelves appear automatically — no configuration needed.
smart_visible_days_empty_warning: No days selected — shelf will not appear
smart_visible_hours_desc: When enabled, this shelf only appears within the time window below.
sort_chain_desc: Earlier rows are the primary order; later rows break ties. Each row has its own asc/desc toggle.
source_store_hint: Browse games from the Steam Store. Apply discount or other filters to narrow results. Requires online features.
source_wishlist_hint: Your Steam wishlist — games you want to buy. Requires online features to be enabled.
template_picker_desc: Select a preset or start from scratch.
settings_statistics_desc: Your library at a glance, read live through the plugin API.
settings_backup_shelves_desc: Export or import only your regular shelves (not smart shelves).
settings_backup_smart_desc: Export or import smart shelf definitions + the surprise-me flag.
settings_backup_all_desc: Export or import every setting, toggle, and saved filter.
settings_advanced_reset_desc: Delete shelves, smart shelves, or every setting. A confirmation modal asks before anything is removed.
settings_advanced_logs_desc: Last 50 plugin events — errors, warnings, and informational entries. Cleared on plugin reload.
settings_profiles_save_desc: Capture every setting + shelf list into a named profile you can restore later.
settings_profiles_list_desc: Apply a profile to swap all settings + shelves to its snapshot. Duplicate to fork, rename to relabel, delete to remove.
settings_profiles_active_desc: The "{{name}}" profile is currently applied. Detaching keeps all your current settings but unties them from any saved profile.
settings_profiles_apply_confirm_message: This replaces every current setting and your shelf list with the profile snapshot. Profiles themselves are preserved.
settings_profiles_delete_confirm_message: The profile snapshot will be removed. This does not affect current settings or shelves.
profile_save_description: Captures every setting, every shelf, and every saved filter into a named snapshot you can restore later.
profile_save_name_taken: A profile with that name already exists.
settings_profiles_io_desc: Move profiles between devices or back them up. Files land in your Downloads folder by default.
binding_help: Customize the button combos that trigger Deck Shelves actions. Reserved buttons (A / B / Menu / Steam / ...) cannot be bound — even in chord. Disable a binding entirely with “Disable” where allowed.
binding_card_quick_launch: Quick launch (Install / Play / Resume / Uninstall / Pause)
settings_export_pick_desc: Pick which sections to include in the export.
settings_import_pick_desc: Pick which sections from this file to apply.
settings_reset_pick_desc: Pick which sections to reset to factory defaults.
settings_backup_custom_desc: Pick which sections to include in the export or import.
settings_statistics_remove_confirm_body: It has no recorded views. You can recreate it anytime.
